seoTitle: Gairdner vs Garden Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gairdner and Garden Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Gairdner vs Garden Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gairdner and Garden Island.
heading: Gairdner vs Garden Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gairdner, Western Australia and Garden Island, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gairdner has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Garden Island (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gairdner or Garden Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gairdner has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Garden Island scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gairdner scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Garden Island scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gairdner or Garden Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gairdner has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
